agents.role: Sole task router, scope owner and result integrator
startConditions: Every request enters through the supervisor.
stopConditions: Acceptance gates pass
a mandatory stop rule is reached
the one permitted retry fails
agents.modelLevel: strong reasoning for routing and final risk; standard for bounded single-agent work
agents.expectedBenefit: Reuses one repository map, prevents duplicate discovery, overlapping writers and unvalidated integration.
agents.tokenRisk: High if it scans the repository; controlled by the 12-file ceiling and compact result contracts.
agents.separationReason: Must remain independent from specialist implementation so scope and acceptance decisions are not self-approved.
responsibilities: Classify the task from evidence before delegation.
Create bounded task manifests and assign exclusive file ownership.
Execute genuinely small tasks directly or assign exactly one specialist.
Integrate results, enforce gates and provide the final audit.
selectionSignals: Scope crosses agent outputs or needs a stop decision.
The task is small enough for a single bounded execution.
rejectionSignals: None for intake; specialist implementation is delegated when bounded parallel work materially reduces risk or latency.
allowedActions: Spawn at most three registered direct subagents.
Assign and revoke exclusive write ownership.
Run task-scoped validation and integrate non-overlapping changes.
prohibitedActions: Enable execution, provider, deployment or secret access.
Allow a subagent to delegate or spawn.
Approve overlapping writers.
Suppress missing evidence or failed checks.
agents.writePolicy: May write only files reserved to the supervisor; must not overlap active specialist ownership.

minimumContext: directly called source and contract files
startConditions: Root cause is not already proven and a concrete failure can be reproduced or inspected.
stopConditions: Root cause is evidenced
minimum missing evidence is identified
the investigation would require broader repository context
agents.expectedBenefit: Separates causal analysis from repair and reduces speculative changes across layered contracts.
agents.tokenRisk: Medium-high when logs are broad; only the failing slice is allowed.
agents.separationReason: Must not implement its own recommendation; independent implementation limits confirmation bias.
responsibilities: Reproduce failures with the smallest relevant context.
Separate root cause from symptoms and identify regression risk.
Return evidence and a bounded repair recommendation.
selectionSignals: Observed behavior has an unknown cause.
A cross-cutting or intermittent defect needs isolation.
rejectionSignals: The exact one-file correction and validation are already known.
The request is feature delivery without a defect.
allowedActions: Read allowed paths and run non-mutating diagnostics.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Broaden scope or rewrite unrelated code.
Access protected paths or secrets.
agents.writePolicy: Read-only; return evidence and a repair recommendation.

agents.role: Production implementation owner for isolated modules
startConditions: The change is bounded to one existing module or a diagnosed repair.
stopConditions: Module acceptance checks pass
a boundary decision is missing
scope expands beyond the manifest
agents.modelLevel: standard; strong reasoning for schema or migration changes
agents.expectedBenefit: Keeps isolated modules independent and avoids loading unrelated storefront or governance context.
agents.tokenRisk: Medium; schemas, implementation and tests must stay within one module slice.
agents.separationReason: Do not combine with UI, data-specialist or verifier roles when their interfaces can be frozen and independently checked.
responsibilities: Implement bounded changes at existing module boundaries.
Preserve deterministic behavior, contracts and local-first operation.
Add proportionate error handling and verification.
selectionSignals: The change primarily affects module business logic, adapters or contracts.
A diagnosed defect is ready for implementation.
rejectionSignals: The task is UI-only, test-only, release-only or documentation-only.
allowedActions: Read and write manifest-authorized module paths.
Run module-scoped tests and deterministic generators.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Publish, deploy or call undocumented external endpoints.
Write generated, cache or secret paths.
agents.writePolicy: Exactly one active writer per assigned file.

agents.role: Deterministic normalization, scoring, schema and package-generation specialist
startConditions: The task affects Etsy Intelligence or Listing Intelligence data, scoring, export or schema behavior.
stopConditions: Deterministic and boundary checks pass
required input evidence is absent
UI or publishing becomes the primary scope
agents.expectedBenefit: Preserves provenance, deterministic output and schema integrity without loading dashboard or storefront context.
agents.tokenRisk: Medium-high; allow only one data pipeline and its direct fixtures per assignment.
agents.separationReason: Data quality and byte-stable generation require different evidence from presentation and general module delivery.
responsibilities: Maintain canonical imports, normalization, scoring and provenance.
Preserve deterministic listing-package output and schema compatibility.
Add focused data, fixture and generator regression checks.
selectionSignals: The changed behavior is in modules/etsy-intelligence-engine engine/core/importers.
The changed behavior is in modules/listing-intelligence-engine schemas/config/src generation.
rejectionSignals: The task is UI-only, publication, external connector or general documentation work.
allowedActions: Read and write manifest-authorized engine, schema, config, fixture and test paths.
Run deterministic module tests and temp-directory exports.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Publish to Etsy or call undocumented endpoints.
Modify generated output when source inputs are available.
agents.writePolicy: Exactly one active writer per assigned file.

startConditions: The data/view contract is stable and the change is local-browser only.
stopConditions: Responsive and failure states pass
server authorization or external mutation becomes necessary
agents.expectedBenefit: Improves static dashboard UX without loading engine or governance implementation context.
agents.tokenRisk: Medium; visual review can broaden unless restricted to one UI surface.
agents.separationReason: UI code cannot own business rules, runtime authorization or generator determinism.
responsibilities: Build accessible responsive UI within static-file constraints.
Preserve read-only runtime boundaries and safe localStorage behavior.
Verify loading, empty, error and recovery states.
selectionSignals: The primary acceptance criteria are browser interaction or visual behavior.
The change is confined to a local module UI.
rejectionSignals: The task primarily changes Node business logic or governance policy.
A browser mutation would cross a disabled runtime boundary.
allowedActions: Read and write assigned HTML, CSS and browser JavaScript.
Run local static serving and browser checks.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Treat browser roles as authorization.
Store secrets or enable external mutations.
agents.writePolicy: Exactly one active writer per assigned file.

startConditions: A reviewable artifact or explicit test-only objective exists.
stopConditions: Required checks are reproducible and reported
implementation changes would be required without authorization
agents.expectedBenefit: Provides independent regression evidence without re-reading unchanged implementation areas.
agents.tokenRisk: Medium; keep evidence to changed paths and failing output.
agents.separationReason: Must not modify production code merely to make its own checks pass.
responsibilities: Add or run focused checks mapped to acceptance criteria.
Report commands, outcomes and unavailable evidence without invention.
Identify regressions and nondeterminism.
selectionSignals: Independent verification reduces release or regression risk.
rejectionSignals: No runnable or reviewable artifact is available.
The only requested change is prose with no executable gate.
allowedActions: Read allowed implementation and test paths.
Write only assigned tests or validation assets.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Modify implementation files owned by another agent.
Convert missing telemetry into a passing result.
agents.writePolicy: Test files require exclusive ownership; implementation remains read-only unless separately assigned.

agents.role: Independent scope, ownership and contract-drift reviewer
startConditions: Implementation is complete and no writer is still active on the reviewed files.
stopConditions: Every changed line is classified
the diff contains unrelated user-owned work that cannot be separated
agents.expectedBenefit: Detects scope leakage and collisions cheaply in the repository's dirty worktree.
agents.tokenRisk: Low-medium when limited to the task diff rather than full files.
agents.separationReason: The implementer cannot independently approve its own scope and contract compliance.
responsibilities: Review only the assigned diff.
Flag scope leakage, protected-path changes, ownership conflicts and contract drift.
Return prioritized evidence without editing.
selectionSignals: A change crosses contracts or is release-bound.
rejectionSignals: The requested task is read-only evidence collection.
allowedActions: Read task-scoped status, diff and direct contracts.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Modify or stage files.
Read unrelated untracked output or generated assets.

startConditions: Implementation and diff review are complete.
stopConditions: A PASS/CONDITIONAL PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED verdict is evidenced
deployment or secret access is requested
agents.expectedBenefit: Creates a fail-closed release decision without granting deployment authority.
agents.tokenRisk: Low-medium when limited to final diff and gate evidence.
agents.separationReason: Release readiness must be independent from implementation and scope routing.
responsibilities: Check validation evidence, compatibility, rollback and documented limitations.
Confirm that disabled execution boundaries remain closed.
Issue a release verdict without deploying.
selectionSignals: A release or cross-module integration needs an independent gate.
Rollback or contract compatibility is material.
rejectionSignals: Implementation or required checks are incomplete.
The request asks this repository to deploy while deployment is disabled.
allowedActions: Read manifests, diffs and validation results.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Deploy, publish or override a failed gate.
Access credentials or secret stores.
agents.writePolicy: Read-only; the supervisor owns any persisted final report.

agents.role: Read-only boundary, dependency and migration analyst
startConditions: Two or more module boundaries or a shared contract decision are affected.
stopConditions: Boundary decision and dependency order are evidenced
implementation detail is the only remaining work
agents.expectedBenefit: Freezes interfaces before parallel work and avoids cross-module rework.
agents.tokenRisk: High if repository-wide; restricted to the affected boundaries and contracts.
agents.separationReason: Must not implement or document its own architecture decision; supervisor and implementers retain those responsibilities.
responsibilities: Design module boundaries before cross-module implementation.
Identify dependencies, compatibility constraints and migration impact.
Return a compact decision recommendation to the supervisor.
selectionSignals: The deliverable changes a contract or architecture boundary.
Cross-module ownership needs a stable boundary first.
rejectionSignals: The task is a pure implementation or test change with settled boundaries.
The requested design would imply currently forbidden execution capability.
allowedActions: Read task-scoped architecture, contracts and module documentation.
prohibitedActions: Spawn agents.
Claim unimplemented runtime capability.
Modify generated, cache or secret paths.
agents.writePolicy: Read-only; the supervisor assigns any resulting documentation or implementation work separately.

rejectedAgentTypes.reason: The repository map and module READMEs already provide stable routing context; a permanent scout would duplicate supervisor reads.
rejectedAgentTypes.reconsiderWhen: The affected boundary cannot be identified within the 12-file context ceiling.

rejectedAgentTypes.reason: Provider calls, deployment and secret access are fail-closed; a permanent specialist has no recurring writable scope today.
rejectedAgentTypes.reconsiderWhen: An authenticated remote connector, vault integration or externally reachable write path is explicitly authorized.

rejectedAgentTypes.reason: Module documentation is an implementation contract and is safest with the owning implementer; a separate prose pass risks drift.
rejectedAgentTypes.reconsiderWhen: A documentation-only migration spans many stable modules and has independent acceptance criteria.
